description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. New module `lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex` — STATELESS module (NO GenServer) operating on an ETS table passed via `cfg.beam_cache_table`. Per mutagen.decision.per_run_beam_cache, the table is created at the start of `MutationRunner.run/1` and deleted in the `after` clause.
   - Table opts: `:ets.new(:beam_cache, [:set, :public, read_concurrency: true])`
   - `BeamCache.snapshot(table, module, code_server)` — idempotent; reads via `code_server.get_object_code(module)`, inserts via `:ets.insert_new/2`
   - `BeamCache.restore(table, module, code_server)` — looks up entry, calls `code_server.load_binary(module, beam_filename, binary)`
2. New module `lib/mutagen_ex/test/code_server.ex` (production impl) — implements the `MutagenEx.Test.CodeServerFacade` behaviour. Production delegates to `:code.get_object_code/1` and `:code.load_binary/3` directly.
3. New behaviour module `lib/mutagen_ex/test/code_server_facade.ex` defining the two callbacks per mutagen.decision.code_server_facade:
   - `@callback get_object_code(module()) :: {module(), binary(), charlist()} | :error`
   - `@callback load_binary(module(), charlist(), binary()) :: {:module, module()} | {:error, term()}`
4. `cfg.code_server` selects the implementation (defaults to `MutagenEx.Test.CodeServer`). Mirrors `cfg.compiler` exactly.
5. Modify `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`:
   a. Create the per-run ETS table.
   b. **Serial snapshot pre-pass BEFORE `async_stream_nolink/4` dispatch.** For every module that any site touches, call `BeamCache.snapshot(table, module, code_server)`. This closes the TOCTOU race documented in 04a finding #1.
   c. **Snapshot runs AFTER `:cover.compile_directory/1`** so the cached binary IS the cover-instrumented binary. Restore preserves coverage instrumentation. Order verified via test.
   d. Replace `restore/3` (mutation_runner.ex:1023) to call `BeamCache.restore(table, module, code_server)` via `:code.load_binary/3`. NO `Code.compile_quoted` in the restore path.
   e. `after` clause: `:ets.delete(cfg.beam_cache_table)` — even on raise/throw/exit. Use `try/after` not just `with_restore`.
6. The `with_restore/4` wrapper (mutation_runner.ex:736-753) shape and semantics are PRESERVED. Its closure now invokes the new restore path internally but the wrapper itself is unchanged.
7. New test `test/mutagen_ex/beam_cache_test.exs`: unit tests using a stub `code_server` that records calls and returns canned responses. Tests cover snapshot idempotency, restore correctness, ETS cleanup on `:ok` AND raise paths.
8. One integration test (tagged `:integration`) that exercises the real `:code` module: snapshot → load_binary a modified binary → restore → assert original bytecode is back.
9. Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md` to document the binary-swap restore (likely a new requirement covering "restore is `:code.load_binary/3`, not `Code.compile_quoted`").

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; `beam_cache_test.exs` green (unit); `mix test --include integration` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate: determinism test green; the original `with_restore/4` failure-mode tests (from `.17`) still pass — restore-failure-during-exception still surfaces correctly

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (do NOT add BeamCache as a child — per mutagen.decision.per_run_beam_cache, no supervisor child)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast.ex (S1 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/ast_cache.ex (S2 owns)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/dispatch.ex (S3 owns)
- Any modification to async_stream_nolink semantics (`.30` territory)
Allowed touches:
- lib/mutagen_ex/beam_cache.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/test/code_server.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/test/code_server_facade.ex (new)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (snapshot pre-pass + restore path + cfg threading; do NOT touch with_restore/4 wrapper shape)
- test/mutagen_ex/beam_cache_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- NO new supervisor child. The application.ex `MutagenEx.Application` shape stays exactly as `.18` set it. Per mutagen.decision.per_run_beam_cache the ETS table is per-run, owned by the `MutationRunner.run/1` process — NOT a supervised singleton.
- NO `Code.compile_quoted` in the restore path. After this stage, restore is `:code.load_binary/3` exclusively. Grep verification: `Code.compile_quoted` should only appear in the mutate path (line ~557) and `safe_compile_quoted/3`, NOT in `restore/3`.
- The `with_restore/4` wrapper signature and external behaviour MUST be byte-identical to pre-`.25`. Its internals can change to call the new restore; its contract cannot.
- The pre-pass snapshot order matters: it MUST run after `:cover.compile_directory/1`. If you snapshot before cover-instrumenting, restore swaps in the uninstrumented binary and coverage breaks.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Determinism test green
- [ ] BeamCache unit tests green
- [ ] BeamCache integration test green (`:integration` tag)
- [ ] `.17`/`.18` failure-mode tests still pass (raise/throw/exit during loaded window triggers restore)
- [ ] `MutagenEx.Application` supervisor child list unchanged (grep verification)
- [ ] `with_restore/4` wrapper shape unchanged (grep verification)
- [ ] No `Code.compile_quoted` calls in `restore/3` (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.05: BeamCache per-run ETS + binary-swap restore`
title: S5: BeamCache per-run ETS + binary-swap restore
